info.title: Heartbeat API
info.description: An API for managing your Heartbeat community

get.description: Get a user by id

404.description: Could not find the specified user

post.description: Reactivate a user. Can be called after calling delete on a user to allow them to access the community again.

email.description: The email of the user to reactivate. If no user with the provided email exists, will return a 404 response.

404.description: Could not find the specified user

get.description: Return an array of all users within a Heartbeat community

put.description: Create a new user in a Heartbeat community

email.description: The user's email. Must be unique to the community

roleID.description: The id of the role the user should have

groupIDs.description: A list of the ids of the groups that the user should belong to

profilePicture.description: A Data URI scheme in the JPG, GIF, or PNG format. Ensure you use the proper content type (image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif) that matches the image data being provided

bio.example: I'm the best salesman at Dunder Mifflin

status.example: Trying to get promoted to Assistant Regional Manager

linkedin.description: A link to the user's LinkedIn profile

twitter.description: A link to the user's Twitter profile

instagram.description: A link to the user's Instagram profile

createIntroductionThread.description: If true and a value for bio is provided, an introduction thread for the user will be created in the channel designated for introductions in your community settings. The introduction thread will include the user's bio

email.description: The email of the user that will be updated. If no user with the provided email exists, will return a 404 response.

profilePicture.description: A Data URI scheme in the JPG, GIF, or PNG format. Ensure you use the proper content type (image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif) that matches the image data being provided

bio.example: I'm the best salesman at Dunder Mifflin

status.example: Trying to get promoted to Assistant Regional Manager

linkedin.description: A link to the user's LinkedIn profile

twitter.description: A link to the user's Twitter profile

instagram.description: A link to the user's Instagram profile

completedLessons.description: Lessons included here will be added to the user's list of completed lessons. This field will never cause a lesson to be removed from the user's list of completed lessons.

404.description: Could not find a user with the provided email address

delete.description: Remove a user from a Heartbeat community. The user will no longer be able to access the community. Threads, comments & messages from the user will not be removed

email.description: The email of the user to be deleted. If no user with the provided email exists, will return a 404 response.

404.description: Could not find a user with the provided email address

put.description: Create a new pending user in a Heartbeat community. A pending user does not yet existing within the community. However, the first time they attempt to log in, they will be created as a user within the community. If this is called for a pending user that already exists, it will update the pending user. If this is called for a user that already exists within the community, it will return an error.

email.description: The user's email. Must be unique to the community

roleID.description: The id of the role the user should have

groupIDs.description: A list of the ids of the groups that the user should belong to

profilePicture.description: A Data URI scheme in the JPG, GIF, or PNG format. Ensure you use the proper content type (image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif) that matches the image data being provided

bio.example: I'm the best salesman at Dunder Mifflin

get.description: Get a user by email

404.description: Could not find a user with a matching email

get.description: Return an array of all the invitations within a Heartbeat community

put.description: Create a new invitation link within a Heartbeat community

roleID.description: Members joining via this invitation link will join as this role

groupIDs.description: Members joining via this invitation link will automatically be in these groups

get.description: Get the number of pending notifications a user in your community has

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address of the user

404.description: Could not find requested user

post.description: Add users to an existing invitation

emails.description: The list of emails to add to the invitation

shouldSendEmail.description: Whether or not to send an invitation email to the users. If false, the users will be allowed to signup via the invitation link, however they will not receive an email from Heartbeat inviting them to join.

404.description: Could not find requested invitation

get.description: Returns an array of all the roles in a Heartbeat community
Returns an array of all Groups within a Heartbeat community

put.description: Create a new Group in a Heartbeat community

members.description: The email addresses of the users that should be added to the group

parentGroupID.description: The id of the parent group

isIsolated.description: Whether the group is isolated. Refer to Heartbeat docs to learn more about what this entails

isJoinable.description: Whether the group is joinable. Refer to Heartbeat docs to learn more about what this entails

get.description: Get a group

404.description: Could not find requested group id

post.description: Edit a group. All fields are optional, only fields that are provided will be changed.

isIsolated.description: Whether the group is isolated. Refer to Heartbeat docs to learn more about what this entails

isJoinable.description: Whether the group is joinable. Refer to Heartbeat docs to learn more about what this entails

400.description: The group cannot be deleted. A reason will be provided

404.description: Could not find requested group id

emails.description: The emails of the users that should be added to the group

shouldRemoveFromSiblingGroups.description: If true, the users will be removed from any other groups that share a parent group with the selected group. Useful when moving users between different stages

404.description: Could not find requested group id or user email

emails.description: The emails of the users that should be removed from the group

404.description: Could not find requested group id or user email

get.description: Returns an array of all channel categories within a community

put.description: Create a new channel category in a Heartbeat community

post.description: Edit an existing channel category in a Heartbeat community

delete.description: Delete an existing channel category in a Heartbeat community

put.description: Create a new voice channel in a Heartbeat community

description.example: A place to discuss anything you want

channelCategoryID.description: The id for the category where the channel should be created

invitedUsers.description: A list of emails corresponding to the users that can access this channel. If the channel is private, only these users will be able to access the voice channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

invitedGroups.description: If the voice channel is private, only these groups will be able to access the channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

post.description: Edit a voice channel. All fields are optional, only fields that are provided will be changed.

description.example: A place to discuss anything you want

restrictedTo.description: An object describing who can access this voice channel. If set to null, then the voice channel will be public.

invitedUsers.description: A list of emails corresponding to the users that can access this voice channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

invitedGroups.description: Only these groups will be able to access the voice channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

get.description: Returns an array of all channels

put.description: Create a new Channel in a Heartbeat community

description.example: A place to discuss anything you want

channelCategoryID.description: The id for the category where the channel should be created

invitedUsers.description: A list of emails corresponding to the users that can access this channel. If the channel is private, only these users will be able to access the channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

invitedGroups.description: If the channel is private, only these groups will be able to access the channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

isReadOnly.description: If true, only admins & moderators will be able to create threads in this channel. This option is not currently available for chat channels

channelType.description: Thread channels are created by default

post.description: Edit a channel. All fields are optional, only fields that are provided will be changed.

description.example: A place to discuss anything you want

restrictedTo.description: An object describing who can access this channel. If set to null, then the channel will be public.

invitedUsers.description: A list of emails corresponding to the users that can access this channel. If the channel is private, only these users will be able to access the channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

invitedGroups.description: If the channel is private, only these groups will be able to access the channel. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are provided, the lists will be combined.

isReadOnly.description: If true, only admins & moderators will be able to create threads in this channel. This option is not currently available for chat channels

get.description: Returns an array of the 20 most recent threads in a channel

404.description: Could not find requested channel id

get.description: Get a thread

404.description: Could not find requested thread

text.description: See the Rich Text page to learn more about formatting

userID.description: The user that will be shown as the author of the thread. Only an admin user can be chosen. If this field is not provided, the user that created the API key will be used

createdAt.description: Optional timestamp to override the default creation time for the thread. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

text.description: See the Rich Text page to learn more about formatting

parentCommentID.description: Heartbeat comments have two levels of nesting. If null is provided, then the comment will be a direct reply to the thread. If a comment id is provided, then the comment will be a reply to that comment. The provided comment id must be a comment that is a direct reply to the thread.

userID.description: The user that will be shown as the author of the comment. Only an admin user can be chosen. If this field is not provided, the user that created the API key will be used

createdAt.description: Optional timestamp to override the default creation time for the comment. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

put.description: Create a new direct chat between two users. If one already exists, then nothing will be created and the existing chat id & URL will be returned.

userID1.description: One of the users in the direct chat

userID2.description: The other user in the direct chat

url.description: A URL pointing to this direct chat

404.description: Could not find a user with a matching user id

text.description: See the Rich Text page to learn more about formatting

to.description: The user id of the receiver.

from.description: The user id of the sender. Only an admin user can be chosen. If this field is not provided, the user that created the API key will be used.

404.description: Could not find a user with a matching user id

get.description: Get the 100 most recent chat messages from a direct message conversation. Only direct messages that include at least one admin user can be accessed via the API.

404.description: Could not find requested direct message

put.description: Send a new chat message to a chat channel

text.description: See the Rich Text page to learn more about formatting

from.description: The user id of the sender. Only an admin user can be chosen. If this field is not provided, the user that created the API key will be used.

createdAt.description: Optional timestamp to override the default creation time for the chat message. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

get.description: Return an array all Events within a Community

parameters.name: groupID
parameters.description: If provided, only events that have been assigned to this group will be returned

404.description: Could not find requested group id

put.description: Create a new Event in a Heartbeat community

description.example: A time for everyone to meet each other & understand what our community is all about

location.description: Where the event will be held. If the string 'HEARTBEAT' is provided, then the event will take place in a Heartbeat voice channel. If the string 'ZOOM' is provided, then the event will take place in a custom Zoom link (this option can only be chosen if a Zoom account has been integrated with Heartbeat). Otherwise, the provided string will be passed directly to the Custom location field.

invitedUsers.description: A list of users that should be invited to the event. If the email matches an existing Heartbeat user, the Heartbeat user will be invited. Otherwise, an event invite will be sent directly to the provided email address. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are empty, the event will be open to everyone in the community.

invitedGroups.description: A list of groups that should be invited to the event. If both invitedUsers & invitedGroups are empty, the event will be open to everyone in the community.

get.description: Get an event

404.description: Could not find requested event id

get.description: Return all the event instances of a recurring Event

404.description: Could not find event id

get.description: Returns attendance data for the last 10 instances of the specified event. If this event is a non-recurring event, an array of size 1 will be returned with attendance data.

id.description: If this attendee was matched to a Heartbeat user, their user id will be populated here.

isUser.description: Whether or not this attendee was matched to a Heartbeat user.

404.description: Could not find event ID

get.description: Get a list of all of your webhooks

url.description: The URL where webhook events will be sent to

id.description: The id of the webhook that was just created

get.description: Get all of the courses in your community
Get a lesson by id

404.description: Could not find the specified lesson

content.description: The content of the lesson (in Markdown)

videoID.description: The id of the Heartbeat video that should be displayed in the hero section

url.description: The URL of an external video to display in the hero section. Youtube, Vimeo & Loom links are supported.

numDays.description: The lesson will become available to users this many days after they start the course

courseID.description: The course where the lesson should be created

cohortID.description: The cohort where the lesson should be created

moduleID.description: The module where the lesson should be created

content.description: The content of the lesson (in Markdown)

videoID.description: The id of the Heartbeat video that should be displayed in the hero section

url.description: The URL of an external video to display in the hero section. Youtube, Vimeo & Loom links are supported.

numDays.description: The lesson will become available to users this many days after they start the course

get.description: Get all of the Heartbeat native videos in your community

aspectRatio.description: The aspect ratio of the video (e.g., "16:9", "4:3").

duration.description: The duration of the video in seconds.

views.description: The number of times the video has been viewed.

get.description: Return an array of all documents (wiki posts) within a Heartbeat community. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: startingAfter
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination - return results after this document ID

get.description: Get a document by ID, including its content

404.description: Could not find requested document

UnauthorizedError.description: API Key is missing or invalid

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the lesson was created.

createdBy.description: User ID of the person who created the lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the community embed card.

title.description: Title of the community embed card.

description.description: Description of the community embed card.

wikiPostID.description: Unique identifier for the wiki post.

userIDs.description: List of unique identifiers for users.

promptDirectMessage.description: Whether course members should be given the option to DM these users

cohorts.description: List of cohorts associated with the course

modules.description: List of modules in the cohort

lessons.description: List of lessons in the module

isAdmin.description: Whether or not the user is an administrator

question.description: The question this user was asked while onboarding

answer.description: This user's answer to the question

isPrivate.description: Whether this question was marked as private

bio.example: I'm the best salesman at Dunder Mifflin

avatar.description: A URL that points to the user's Heartbeat avatar

linkedInSummary.description: A single formatted string containing the user's entire LinkedIn history. Empty if no LinkedIn data is available

headline.example: Ready to crush your sales quotas

description.example: I'm a motivated salesman always willing to go the extra mile
Designated committee to plan holiday, birthday, & retirement parties

color.format: The hex code color of the group

parentGroupID.description: The id of the group's parent. Returns null if the group has no parent

archived.description: Whether the group has been archived or not

description.example: The best event to happen this year. Look forward to fireworks and BBQ!

startTime.description: The original start time for the event in UTC. If this event is recurring, this might be in the past.

endTime.description: The original end time for the event in UTC. If this event is recurring, this may be in the past.

createdAt.description: The date and time in UTC at which the event was created.

createdBy.description: The user id of the person that created the event

invitedUsers.description: The users that are invited to this event

invitedGroups.description: The groups that are invited to this event

code.description: A 6 digit alphanumeric code included within the invitation link. Invitation links use the following format: https://app.heartbeat.chat/example/invitation?code=<CODE> OR https://example.customdomain.com/invitation?code=<CODE>

type.description: What type of channel this is.

name.description: The name of the channel cateogry

channelID.description: The id of the channel where this thread exists

userID.description: The id of the user that created this thread

files.description: A list of URLs pointing to any file attachments on the thread

url.description: A URL pointing to this thread

userID.description: The id of the user that created this comment

images.description: A list of URLs pointing to any image attachments on the comment

files.description: A list of URLs pointing to any file attachments on the comment

children.description: A list of child comments. Heartbeat comments only have 2 levels of nesting, so a child comment will never have more child comments underneath

userID.description: The id of the user that created this comment

images.description: A list of URLs pointing to any image attachments on the comment

files.description: A list of URLs pointing to any file attachments on the comment

userID.description: The id of the user that created this chat message

content.description: The content of the chat message

images.description: A list of URLs pointing to any image attachments on the comment

files.description: A list of URLs pointing to any file attachments on the comment

WebhookAction.description: Which actions in your Heartbeat community should trigger this webhook. See the Webhooks page to learn more about each action

triggerOnMove.description: If enabled, this webhook will trigger when threads are moved into the specified channel

link.description: A URL pointing to this document in the Heartbeat community
A URL pointing to this document in the Heartbeat community

content.description: The markdown content of the document
